rules.rule: A hook may manage React lifecycle and state. This is its primary permitted responsibility.
A hook must not replace a foundation service. Fetching and filtering data with business rules must move to services.
A hook must not contain core business rules.
A page hook may call a view service injected as a parameter — not instantiated inside the hook.
Effects that perform async work must use a mounted flag or AbortController and clean up in the effect return.
Do not use useEffect for data transformation. Use view services or render-time derivation instead.
Do not suppress hook dependency warnings without a documented reason.
